key_insight: Using the reverse Fisher divergence instead of the forward Fisher divergence makes gradient descent globally convergent for fitting Gaussian mixtures in settings where the standard objective can fail or depend on initialization.
engineering_relevance: This gives a more reliable training objective for score matching in generative modeling, diffusion models, and inverse problems when optimizing mixture-based or unnormalized density models.
method: Analyze GD on Gaussian mixture student models with fixed weights and identity covariances.
Use a Lyapunov-based argument to prove monotone descent and global convergence.
Extend the proof to teacher Gaussian mixtures under random initialization and mean-separation assumptions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports global convergence guarantees, including high-probability component matching and total-variation convergence under a \widetilde\Omega(1)-separation condition.
watch_item: The strongest guarantees require fixed covariances/weights for the student and a separation assumption on the teacher mixture means, so performance may be weaker in overlapping or fully learned-mixture settings.
paper_title: Global Convergence of Gradient Descent for Score Matching in Gaussian Mixtures via Reverse Fisher Divergence
